description_long: This apartment building is a modern high-rise structure with a distinctive curved facade, featuring two semi-cylindrical towers joined at the center. The exterior is dominated by large, horizontally-aligned glass windows that wrap around each floor, separated by prominent white bands. The ground level includes a dark, rectangular base with a main entrance and a series of steps leading up to it, flanked by landscaped green areas with geometric patterns. The building's roof is flat and follows the curved outline of the towers, giving it a sleek and contemporary appearance. The overall design emphasizes symmetry and repetition, with the window bands and facade elements creating a rhythmic visual effect. The structure appears to be constructed primarily from concrete, glass, and steel, typical of urban residential towers.
description: A modern, high-rise apartment building with curved twin towers, large wraparound glass windows, and a sleek, symmetrical facade. The ground level features a prominent entrance and landscaped green areas.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, landscaped areas, and the curved glass facade with visible window bands.
description_view_1: Rear-side view highlighting the continuous curved glass facade and the building's solid base, with less detail on the entrance.
description_view_2: Back view focusing on the twin curved towers and the flat roof, with minimal landscaping visible.
description_view_3: Angled front view displaying both the entrance and the geometric landscaping, as well as the building's overall symmetry.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in continuous horizontal bands wrapping around each floor, creating a symmetrical and repetitive pattern across the curved facade.
